seo.title: CrewAI Guardrails for Legal
seo.description: Compliance guide for CrewAI agents in legal. Meet Attorney-client privilege protections, Bar association ethics rules requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Legal teams using CrewAI face heightened risks due to the framework's multi-agent delegation, which can lead to task drift, hallucinated legal citations, and breaches of attorney-client privilege. Without compliance guardrails, autonomous agents may reinterpret objectives, propagate fabricated case law, or expose confidential data, inviting malpractice suits and bar sanctions. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure AI outputs align with legal ethics and regulations.

complianceRequirements.description: All client communications and data must remain confidential and not be disclosed or used in AI processing without safeguards.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans agent outputs for PII and privileged content, blocking transmission between crew members and enforcing data isolation.

complianceRequirements.description: AI-generated legal advice must be reviewed by a licensed attorney and cannot substitute professional judgment.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex flags unreviewed AI content with metadata tags requiring human attorney sign-off before final outputs.

complianceRequirements.description: All citations must reference real, verifiable cases; fabricated citations violate rules and lead to sanctions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies citations against legal databases, rejecting hallucinated cases like the infamous Mata v. Avianca precedent.

complianceRequirements.description: AI tools in discovery must preserve chain of custody and avoid altering or hallucinating document relevance.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex audits task chains for consistency, detecting drift or fabricated evidence summaries in agent handoffs.

industryRisks.name: Agent delegation loops
industryRisks.description: Crew members endlessly delegate tasks without resolution, delaying critical filings.
industryRisks.example: Research agent loops with drafting agent on contract review, missing court deadline.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated tool outputs
industryRisks.description: Agents pass fabricated case law between roles, leading to erroneous briefs.
industryRisks.example: Citing non-existent 'Mata v. Avianca' in motion, resulting in sanctions.

industryRisks.name: Task drift
industryRisks.description: Agents reinterpret objectives, generating off-topic or unethical advice.
industryRisks.example: Discovery agent shifts to irrelevant plaintiff social media analysis.

industryRisks.name: Inconsistent output quality
industryRisks.description: Variable agent performance leads to mismatched legal arguments across documents.
industryRisks.example: Research agent provides strong precedents, but drafter ignores them for weaker cases.

implementation.title: Instrument CrewAI Tasks with Vex Callback
implementation.description: Add Vex as a task callback to verify outputs for privilege breaches and citation accuracy before delegation.
implementation.code:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from vex_sdk import VexClient

vex = VexClient(api_key='your-vex-key')

def vex_callback(task_output):
    result = vex.verify({
        'content': task_output,
        'policy': 'legal-privilege',
        'checks': ['pii_detection', 'citation_verification']
    })
    if not result['pass']:
        raise ValueError(f'Compliance failure: {result["issues"]}')
    return task_output

research_task = Task(
    description='Research precedents for contract breach.',
    agent=research_agent,
    callback=vex_callback
)

implementation.title: Prevent Delegation Loops with Vex Cycle Detection
implementation.description: Use Vex to monitor crew execution for loops and enforce max delegation depth in legal workflows.
implementation.code:
from crewai import Crew
from vex_sdk import VexClient

vex = VexClient(api_key='your-vex-key')

crew = Crew(
    agents=[research_agent, draft_agent],
    tasks=[research_task, draft_task],
    verbose=True
)

# Vex monitors execution
result = vex.monitor_crew_execution(
    crew_exec=crew.kickoff,
    max_cycles=5,
    policy='legal-no-loops'
)
if result['cycle_detected']:
    print('Loop prevented; escalating to attorney review.')

implementation.title: Enforce Attorney Review on Final Outputs
implementation.description: Vex tags all crew outputs requiring licensed attorney review before court submission.
implementation.code:
from crewai import Crew
from vex_sdk import VexClient

vex = VexClient(api_key='your-vex-key')

crew_result = crew.kickoff()

review_tag = vex.apply_review_tag({
    'content': crew_result,
    'domain': 'legal',
    'requires': ['licensed_attorney'],
    'regulation': 'bar_ethics'
})

if review_tag['status'] == 'pending_review':
    print('Output quarantined for attorney review.')
else:
    print('Compliance verified; ready for filing.')

caseStudyScenario.heading: CrewAI in Legal: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: A mid-sized law firm deploys CrewAI to accelerate eDiscovery in a class-action lawsuit involving 50,000 documents. The crew consists of a Research Agent scanning for precedents, a Discovery Agent categorizing relevant evidence, and a Drafting Agent preparing motions. Without guardrails, the Research Agent hallucinates a key precedent ('Smith v. CorpX, 2023'), which the Drafting Agent incorporates into a filing. Meanwhile, the Discovery Agent begins drifting, pulling irrelevant plaintiff social media data, risking privilege breaches. A delegation loop emerges when agents bounce unclear relevance criteria back and forth, delaying production by 48 hours.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: With Vex integrated as task callbacks, the hallucinated 'Smith v. CorpX' citation is flagged and rejected during Research-to-Drafting handoff, verified against Westlaw. Privilege scans block social media PII transmission. Cycle detection terminates the loop after 3 delegations, escalating to attorney review. The motion is filed accurately with verified citations, avoiding Mata v. Avianca-style sanctions and completing discovery 24 hours early.

cta.heading: Comply with Legal Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex into your CrewAI legal workflows today to prevent hallucinations, enforce privilege, and ensure bar-compliant outputs.
